data.body:
PASS 1 / A1 (anti-slop / over-engineering / NIH) — Cluster 1 canonical-JSON consolidation: CLEAN, no findings.

Verified against the codebase:
- Rule-of-Three PASSES: `_store/canonical.py` already has 6 positional importers (`event_append`, `_commands/{txn,delete,compact}`, `rebar_reconciler/{inbound_translate,batch_dispatch}`) plus the 4 duplicate sites being migrated — well above the ≥3 bar; not premature abstraction.
- NIH-avoidance PASSES: the plan consolidates ONTO the existing seam rather than rebuilding it; the 4 duplicates are confirmed real and near-identical (signing.py:189, llm/workflow/schema.py:396, conflict_resolver.py:218, mutation.py:106).
- Config-surface / golden-hammer PASSES: the two new keyword-only params (`ascii_only`, `default`) are each grounded in a verified per-site byte requirement rather than forcing one form everywhere — conflict_resolver.py omits `ensure_ascii` (stdlib default True) and uses `default=str`; mutation.py uses `ensure_ascii=True`. The plan correctly preserves these divergences instead of normalizing.
- YAGNI PASSES: both params serve current migration sites, not hypotheticals.
